name: ReFi Design Sprint - Batch 01
leads: Raffaele Izzia (lead facilitator); Lorenzo Di Ciaccio (Ridaje, beneficiary)
place: Veracura Space, Rome — Italy's capital and its dense Third Sector fabric
tagline: Four days that turned a gardener's data into public proof.
whyItMattered: Ridaje does real ecological and social work — maintaining Rome's green spaces while employing people rebuilding their lives — but that impact stayed locked in a manual process, invisible to funders. The sprint built infrastructure to make it visible, verifiable, and fundable.
story: The first thing ReFi Italia did after the December 2025 payout was not run a sprint — it was research. A distributed Italian team spent months adapting the Google Ventures sprint for non-crypto-native social cooperatives, in Italian, on tiny budgets. In January they selected Ridaje, a Rome cooperative maintaining 19 green spaces while employing people leaving homelessness and incarceration.

The sprint ran February 24-27, 2026 at Veracura Space: 18 hours over four days. They mapped Ridaje's value chain and confronted a hard constraint — gardeners would not change their five-minute routine — splitting data into off-chain (timestamped) and on-chain (validated). Lorenzo Di Ciaccio, Ridaje's founder, was the Decider; every critical call was his.

Within days of closing, ReFi Italia released OpenGarden — an open-source, EAS-native attestation protocol never named in the original plan. The same team that facilitated the design built the protocol, so four days turned directly into working infrastructure. A $4,630 community grant funded ~140 hours of paid gardening: real jobs, real green space, and the first verified impact data.

Today one of two planned sprints is complete; the second (a rural reforestation and solidarity-purchasing group near Verona) is selected and in planning. The delivered work is solid and on-chain verified. Most of the headline impact — the dashboard, mainnet deployment, enterprise interest — is still honestly labelled as emerging.

signatureStory.title: The DAJE voucher that nobody knew about
signatureStory.text: Before the sprint, Lorenzo had already tried to solve one of Ridaje's core problems alone: how to connect the green spaces his gardeners maintain to the local economy around them. He printed DAJE paper vouchers, partnered with restaurants near Ridaje's parks, then stood outside Stazione Termini convincing tourists to eat at those restaurants using the vouchers. Scrappy, personal, entirely analog — but it worked well enough to prove the willingness to participate in local exchange existed, among businesses, citizens, and visitors alike. Nobody on the sprint team knew DAJE existed. It surfaced in passing during the Day 1 stakeholder mapping exercise, and it reshuffled the sprint's priorities on the spot: community engagement was elevated over ESG credits, because the foundation for neighbourhood-level economic circulation was not hypothetical — it was already being tested on the ground. The surprise reshaped both the sprint and the methodology: communities have more infrastructure than they can fit in an intake form.

delivered: Ran one high-touch 1:1 design sprint with Ridaje (Rome social cooperative) — 18 hours over 4 days, Feb 24-27 2026 at Veracura Space — preceded by a substantial pre-sprint research phase
Deployed and operated an on-chain program treasury (Gnosis Safe) that held the LF matching plus co-funding and made every disbursement — all verified on-chain
Disbursed a $4,630 cUSD community grant to a Ridaje-controlled Safe (3 Mar 2026), funding ~140 hours of paid gardening work plus dashboard development
Released OpenGarden — an open-source, EAS-native attestation protocol with a public GitHub repo and TypeScript SDK — built fast after the sprint and not in the original application
Onboarded Lorenzo Di Ciaccio onto MetaMask and a Safe multisig, and ran EAS attestation activity on Optimism Sepolia testnet (155 attestations made; the report under-claims this as 49)
Pooled ~$9,003 in cash co-funding from five aligned partners, slightly above the $8,500 committed; Sprint 2 (GAS Bovolone / Radici in Movimento) selected and in planning

outcomes.text: Ridaje gained on-chain infrastructure it controls — an open verification protocol designed around its operations, a wallet and multisig, and access to web3 funding ecosystems previously closed to it
~140 hours of paid gardening work for people in social reinsertion is underway, generating the first real verified impact data
A replicable sprint methodology and an open-source protocol now exist, with a cross-territorial network of 7 organisations across Rome, Sicily, and Veneto
A reporting dashboard meant to cut Lorenzo's admin from 8-12h to under 2h/month, plus mainnet deployment and enterprise interest, remain to be validated

status: Active and early — one sprint delivered and verified, one in planning; most impact still emerging

verdict.line: Solid — a credible, on-chain-verified first sprint and a genuine open-source protocol, honestly reported; the main clarifications needed are the community-grant recipient's identity, conflict-of-interest disclosure, and the absent Declaration.

honesty: Every load-bearing claim verifies: the financial flows on independent block explorers, OpenGarden in the public repo, the attestations on testnet — and the team notably under-claimed its own attestation count. The honest caveats are that this is one of two planned sprints, the community-grant recipient address could not be independently tied to Ridaje, conflict of interest went unaddressed (the operator was both facilitator and a co-funder), and most outcomes are still emerging rather than realized.

proposed.summary: Productise the Rifai Sicilia pilot into a repeatable high-touch onboarding sprint for established non-crypto-native Italian regenerative organisations.
points: Run facilitated 5-day Google-Ventures-style design sprints with 2-3 pilot communities
Deploy an on-chain Gnosis Safe treasury and onboard local leaders onto wallets
Give a seed grant as first capital in a community-governed treasury
Produce 1-2 case studies and playbooks, and track in-kind co-funding via Grassroots Economics vouchers

correlation.outcome: One sprint delivered (Ridaje, 18hrs over 4 days); a second is selected and in planning. Format shortened, disclosed.

correlation.proposed: Deploy on-chain Gnosis Safe treasury, onboard local leaders
correlation.outcome: Program Safe deployed and operated (verified on-chain); Lorenzo Di Ciaccio onboarded onto MetaMask and a Ridaje multisig.

correlation.proposed: Seed grant as first capital in a community treasury
correlation.outcome: $4,630 cUSD sent to a Ridaje-controlled Safe (3 Mar 2026), funding ~140 paid gardening hours; transfer verified on-chain.
Pilot 1 Report, a playbook folder, and best-practices docs exist; methodology documented but not yet a polished public case study.

correlation.proposed: Track in-kind co-funding via Grassroots Economics vouchers
correlation.outcome: Voucher commitment pool dropped and in-kind co-funding (Rifai Sicilia, YOMI) vanished; all co-funding was cash, undisclosed in changes.
~$9,003 cash co-funding from five partners (above the $8,500 hard commit but below target); plan scaled to two sprints.

team.role: Impact and Scaling Support, Veracura Network — hosted the sprint at Veracura Space in Rome and contributed to impact framing and post-sprint scaling strategy

disbursements.purpose: Sprint 1 venue, logistics, food and beverage
Community grant — ~140 hours of paid gardening work plus impact dashboard development and follow-up grant research

stories.title: The DAJE voucher that nobody knew about
stories.text: Before the sprint, Lorenzo had already printed DAJE paper vouchers, partnered with restaurants near Ridaje's parks, and stood outside Stazione Termini convincing tourists to eat there. It surfaced in passing during Day 1 mapping and reshuffled the sprint's priorities on the spot.

stories.title: The discovery that reshaped the methodology
stories.text: The team had filed community engagement as distant future work and asked about prior experiments in the intake form. DAJE never came up there — it emerged in the room. That single moment elevated community circulation over ESG credits and changed how ReFi Italia runs sprints.
stories.quote: Communities have more infrastructure than they can articulate in a form.

stories.title: Four days that became working infrastructure
stories.text: Within days of the sprint closing, ReFi Italia released OpenGarden — an open-source, EAS-native attestation protocol never named in the original plan. Because the same team that facilitated the design built the protocol, four compressed days turned directly into a public good rather than a report awaiting implementation.

dimensionEvidence.evidence: The community grant funds ~140 hours of direct urban green-space care across Ridaje's 25,000 m² and 19 Rome sites, and OpenGarden makes ecological stewardship verifiable on-chain as a reusable standard. Independently scored 3 — deliberate ecological support, though measurable outcomes remain emerging (protocol on testnet, the grant a small slice of Ridaje's work).

The grant funds paid work for people in social reinsertion (Ridaje has trained 70+, employed 18, reintegrated 9), and the sprint bridged previously disconnected organisations across Rome, Sicily, and Veneto. Independently scored 3 — a real strength, but durable persisting partnerships are not yet proven and the 70/18/9 figures are Ridaje's track record, not grant outcomes.

OpenGarden is an open-source, chain-agnostic protocol built on EAS; a Safe multisig was deployed and Lorenzo trained hands-on, with blockchain kept invisible to gardeners. Independently scored 3 (matching the self-score) — open-source tools adapted to local needs, with community uptake still emerging and single-person dependency flagged.

timeline.what: Call for Pilots published across Italy
Ridaje selected as first sprint community
Four-day design sprint at Veracura Space, Rome

resources.label: Design Sprint best practices & case studies

operatorChecks: Confirm the Safe 0xCCC0710f…Ebdb24 that received the $4,630 is Ridaje-controlled via a signer list or public reference.
Resolve the conflict-of-interest disclosure gap: ReFi Italia was paid facilitation from the treasury while also a co-funder.
Obtain the completed and signed Declaration section, which is absent from the submitted report.
Confirm whether the Grassroots Economics voucher pool and Rifai Sicilia/YOMI in-kind co-funding were dropped, and why.
Reconcile the team: Antonio Paglino (named Technical Steward) is absent, and the 1.3 headcount lists two uncredited names.
Confirm the community-grant token: it moved as cUSD on Celo but parts of the report say USDC.
